@@ -321,6 +321,7 @@ {"id":"bd-75ja","title":"Quotient Filters for Fast Duplicate Edge Detection","description":"## Quotient Filters for Fast Duplicate Edge Detection Use quotient filters (a cache-friendly alternative to Bloom filters) to detect duplicate edges during graph construction, enabling O(1) amortized duplicate detection with minimal memory overhead. ## Mathematical Foundation A quotient filter stores a set of fingerprints compactly using quotienting: for hash h(x), split into quotient q and remainder r where h(x) = q * 2^r_bits + r. Store remainder r in slot q of a compact array, using linear probing for collisions. Properties: - Space: ~10-25% less than Bloom filters at the same false positive rate - Cache behavior: elements stored in contiguous sorted runs → excellent locality - False positive rate: 2^{-r_bits} (configurable via remainder size) - No false negatives: if element was inserted, it will be found **For duplicate edge detection:** When parsing a Mermaid diagram, each edge (u, v) must be checked for duplicates. For n edges: - Naive HashSet: O(n) space, O(1) per lookup, but HashSet overhead per entry - Quotient filter: O(n * r_bits / 8) bytes, O(1) per lookup, much lower constant factor - For r_bits = 16: false positive rate = 1/65536, space = 2 bytes per edge - For 10000 edges: quotient filter = ~20KB vs HashSet = ~400KB (20x savings) **Practical consideration:** The space savings are significant for WASM target where memory is constrained. For native target, the HashSet overhead is negligible. This is primarily a WASM optimization. ## Key Papers - Bender et al., "Don't Thrash: How to Cache Your Hash on Flash" (VLDB 2012) — quotient filter - Pandey et al., "A General-Purpose Counting Filter" (SIGMOD 2017) — counting quotient filter - Fan et al., "Cuckoo Filter: Practically Better Than Bloom" (CoNEXT 2014) — alternative approach ## Implementation in FrankenMermaid 1. Implement `QuotientFilter` generic over hash function. 2. Use FxHash for fingerprint computation (fast, non-cryptographic). 3. Implement `insert(edge_hash)` and `may_contain(edge_hash)` operations. 4. On `may_contain() == true`: verify with exact edge comparison (handle false positives). 5. Replace `HashSet<(NodeId, NodeId)>` in parser with `QuotientFilter` for WASM target. 6. Keep `HashSet` for native target (simpler, negligible space overhead). 7. Benchmark: memory usage and parse time for 1000, 10000, 100000 edge graphs on WASM. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] QuotientFilter implementation with configurable false positive rate - [ ] Duplicate edge detection correct (no false negatives, false positives handled) - [ ] Memory reduction >= 10x vs HashSet for 10000-edge graphs - [ ] Parse time comparable to HashSet (within 10%) - [ ] WASM-specific optimization (cfg-gated) - [ ] Property test: no false negatives over 100000 random edge sets - [ ] Decision contract: adopt for WASM if memory reduction > 5x","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:46:30.205093497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T21:06:01.445943021Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T21:06:01.445547402Z","close_reason":"Implemented QuotientFilter in fm-core/src/quotient_filter.rs with: configurable q/r bit widths, FxHash-based fingerprinting, linear probing with sorted runs, insert/may_contain O(1) amortized operations, with_capacity auto-sizing (75% load target), memory/FP rate introspection. 14 unit tests all passing: basic operations, duplicates, edge tuples, stress testing (1000 elements, zero false negatives), empirical FP rate verification, memory comparison vs HashSet. All quality gates pass (check, clippy, fmt).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","parser","probabilistic","quotient-filter"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-75ja","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.655486068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-75ja","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:52.362224276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"bd-77gh","title":"Implement IrStyleDef and IrInlineStyle types in fm-core for styling support","description":"# Styling Infrastructure in fm-core ## Purpose Multiple diagram types need styling support (classDef, style, linkStyle directives). This task creates the shared IR types that all styling features depend on. ## Types to Add 1. IrStyleDef { name: String, properties: BTreeMap } — for classDef definitions 2. IrInlineStyle(BTreeMap) — for style/linkStyle directives 3. Add style_defs: Vec to MermaidDiagramIr 4. Add inline_style: Option to IrNode 5. Add inline_style: Option to IrEdge ## SVG Property Mapping Document the mapping from CSS-like property names to SVG attributes: - fill → rect/path fill attribute - stroke → rect/path stroke attribute - stroke-width → stroke-width attribute - stroke-dasharray → stroke-dasharray - color → text fill attribute - font-size → font-size - font-weight → font-weight ## Security - Sanitize all property values to prevent SVG injection - Reject url() values, javascript:, and event handlers ## This unblocks: classDef task, style/linkStyle task, and any diagram-specific styling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:40.857345936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:12:33.964746710Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:12:33.964452402Z","close_reason":"Implemented IrStyleDef, IrInlineStyle types in fm-core with populate_structured_styles(), sanitize_style_value(), parse_style_string() helpers, and 20+ unit tests. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","infrastructure","parity"],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-77gh","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"}]} {"id":"bd-79sj","title":"Implement class relationship cardinality and namespace grouping","description":"# Class Cardinality and Namespace Grouping ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` classDiagram namespace Animals { class Dog class Cat } Dog "1" --> "*" Cat : chases Vehicle "1" *-- "0..*" Wheel \`\`\` - Cardinality labels: "1", "*", "0..1", "1..*", "0..*" on relationship ends - Namespace blocks group classes visually (like packages in UML) - Relationship types: inheritance (<|--), composition (*--), aggregation (o--), association (-->), dependency (..>), realization (..|>) ## Current State - Cardinality labels on relationships: NOT parsed - Namespace blocks: NOT parsed - All relationship arrow types ARE parsed ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Parse quoted cardinality labels before/after relationship arrows. Parse \`namespace Name { ... }\` blocks as clusters. 2. **IR**: Add source_cardinality/target_cardinality: Option to IrEdge. Namespaces map to IrCluster. 3. **SVG Renderer**: Render cardinality labels near edge endpoints. Render namespaces as labeled boundary boxes. 4. **Tests**: Various cardinality labels, namespace grouping, nested namespaces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:44.983780008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:36:01.062205308Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:36:01.061927472Z","close_reason":"Added source_cardinality/target_cardinality fields to IrEdge, parser strips quoted cardinality labels from class diagram edges, namespace blocks create subgraphs+clusters, SVG renderer draws cardinality text near edge endpoints. 6 new tests, all quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["class","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":151,"issue_id":"bd-79sj","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":198,"issue_id":"bd-79sj","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: Class diagrams support a 'direction' keyword just like flowcharts: ``` classDiagram direction RL class A A --> B ``` The parser should recognize 'direction LR/RL/TB/BT' within classDiagram and set ir.direction accordingly. This is likely already handled by the generic direction parsing, but verify and add a test.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:51Z"}]} +{"id":"bd-7e9z","title":"Sanitize themeVariables color overrides for SVG CSS","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:55:02.023978529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:02:42.587160560Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:02:42.586936537Z","close_reason":"Sanitized themeVariables colors in SVG theme overrides","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"bd-7g6d","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram notes","description":"# Sequence Diagram Notes ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` sequenceDiagram Note left of Alice: Single line note Note right of Bob: Another note Note over Alice,Bob: Spanning note Note over Alice: Multi-line
note text \`\`\` - \`Note left of Participant:\` — note box to the left of lifeline - \`Note right of Participant:\` — note box to the right of lifeline - \`Note over Participant:\` — note box centered over lifeline - \`Note over P1,P2:\` — note spanning between two participants - Notes have a distinctive folded-corner box shape ## Current State - NOT parsed. Note lines produce "unsupported sequence syntax" warnings. - No SequenceStatement variant for notes. ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::Note { position: NotePosition, participants: Vec, text: String }. Parse \`Note left of\`, \`Note right of\`, \`Note over\` with participant name(s) and colon-delimited text. 2. **IR**: Store notes in a Vec on MermaidDiagramIr or in an IrSequenceMeta struct. 3. **Layout**: Notes occupy vertical space like messages. Position note boxes at the appropriate x (left/right/over participant) and y (between messages). 4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw note boxes with folded corner (small triangle in top-right). Add text with word wrapping. 5. **Tests**: Left note, right note, over single, over span, multi-line.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.583994264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:06:14.640336586Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:06:14.639876235Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence diagram note parsing. Parser recognizes 'Note left of/right of/over' syntax with single or spanning participants. Multi-line notes via
tags. Notes stored in IrSequenceMeta.notes via IrBuilder.add_sequence_note(). 6 parser tests added covering all note positions, spanning, multiline, and warning behavior. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7g6d","depends_on_id":"bd-6zd9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:25.003211304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":145,"issue_id":"bd-7g6d","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"}]} {"id":"bd-7ib6","title":"Implement markdown labels in node text (backtick syntax)","description":"# Markdown Labels in Nodes ## What mermaid-js Supports \`\`\` flowchart LR A["\`**Bold** and *italic* text\`"] B["\`Multi line text\`"] \`\`\` Backtick-quoted labels support basic markdown: bold, italic, strikethrough, code, and line breaks. ## Current State - Parser handles quoted labels (single/double quotes) - Backtick markdown syntax is NOT parsed — backticks are treated as part of the label text - SVG renderer does not render any markdown formatting in labels ## Implementation Plan 1. **Parser**: Detect backtick-enclosed labels. Strip backticks and parse inline markdown to a simple AST (bold, italic, code spans, line breaks). 2. **IR**: Add IrLabelSegment enum (Plain, Bold, Italic, Code, LineBreak) and change IrLabel.text to IrLabel.segments: Vec (with backward compat: if segments is empty, fall back to text). 3. **SVG Renderer**: Render segments as elements with appropriate font-weight/font-style attributes. Line breaks become separate or elements. 4. **Tests**: Bold, italic, code, mixed, multi-line.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.880498311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T01:52:39.043115692Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T01:52:39.042625725Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":143,"issue_id":"bd-7ib6","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory) This bead MUST ship with: ### Unit Tests - Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases) - Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure) - Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values ### Integration Tests - Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs - Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs) - Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics ### E2E Validation Script Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason. ### Quality Gates All must pass before this bead can be closed: - cargo check --workspace --all-targets - cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings - cargo fmt --check - cargo test --workspace (zero new failures) ","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:40Z"},{"id":200,"issue_id":"bd-7ib6","author":"ubuntu","text":"ADDITIONAL: In addition to markdown backtick labels, mermaid-js also supports HTML entity codes in labels: A["Entity ♥ codes"] renders with ♥. The parser should decode common HTML entities (&#NNN; numeric and &name; named) when processing label text. This can be done in the same label-processing pass as markdown parsing.","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]} {"id":"bd-7l9","title":"EPIC: Project Foundation & Workspace Setup","description":"Top-level epic for all foundational project setup: Cargo workspace, CI/CD, toolchain config, crate skeleton, AGENTS.md update. The FrankenMermaid project extracts the mermaid diagram engine from FrankenTUI (/dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid*.rs, ~40K lines of Rust) into a standalone library with CLI and WASM/canvas web targets. Goal: compete with mermaid-js by rendering ALL diagram types beautifully, handling cycles gracefully, being more forgiving of malformed input, using superior layout algorithms, and providing extensive visual polish controls. ## Success Criteria - Every capability listed in this epic is delivered completely through child issues; no scope cuts and no loss of planned functionality. - Child tasks include comprehensive unit/integration coverage and e2e coverage with detailed logging; test evidence is available and reproducible. - Performance, determinism, and reliability expectations for this epic are validated with measurable checks. - User-facing behavior is documented (README/docs/config/migration notes) for all newly shipped functionality. - Release readiness is proven by passing workspace quality gates and epic-level validation flows end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:27:31.771065629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:22:03.391575126Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T01:22:03.391079037Z","close_reason":"All foundation tasks complete: workspace, toolchain, and documentation established. 8 crates, 953+ tests, full CI gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","foundation"],"comments":[{"id":40,"issue_id":"bd-7l9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space refinement (2026-02-12): replaced coarse epic-level blockers with concrete task-level dependencies (e.g., 3gq/2b4/2u0/2hq chains) to reduce artificial gating, and rebalanced P0 overload so extraction critical path (bd-3bc.*) is clearer. No functionality removed; scope preserved.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:42:23Z"},{"id":41,"issue_id":"bd-7l9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Second plan-space dependency refinement (2026-02-12): shifted several epics off monolithic gate bd-3bc.5 onto concrete extraction prerequisites (bd-3bc.2 / bd-3bc.3 and downstream renderer prerequisites). This preserves quality-gate intent while improving sequencing and reducing artificial critical-path coupling.","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:50:14Z"}]}
